hypothesis: If a diffusion model trained exclusively on metastable state data is guided during inference using interpolated conditional scores (Score-Based Interpolation) and refined via energy-maximizing force projection along the score-difference reaction coordinate (Score-Aligned Ascent), then it will generate geometrically and energetically accurate transition state structures without requiring any transition state training data, because the interpolated score fields implicitly encode the reaction pathway between metastable states and the ascent procedure drives configurations toward saddle points along the most probable transition coordinate.
